description_long: This is a rectangular wooden picnic table with two long benches attached on either side, constructed from evenly spaced wooden planks. The tabletop and benches are supported by sturdy wooden legs and diagonal braces, providing stability and strength. The wood appears weathered and stained, with visible reddish-brown handprint marks on the surface, suggesting heavy use or intentional decoration. The table is designed for communal seating, typical of public parks or outdoor recreational areas. The structure is robust, with thick planks and solid joinery, making it suitable for outdoor environments. There is no visible hole in the center of the tabletop for an umbrella.
description: A weathered wooden picnic table with attached benches and visible reddish-brown stains, designed for outdoor communal seating.
description_view_0: Front view showing the length of the table and benches, with visible handprint stains on the tabletop and bench.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the tabletop and bench planks, as well as the supporting legs and braces.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the full length of the benches and tabletop with additional stains.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the bench and tabletop surfaces, with prominent handprint stains visible.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by thick wooden legs at each end, reinforced by diagonal crossbeams and horizontal braces connecting the legs for added stability.
